content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (the less common British English spelling of reflection, from its high school vocabulary entry) can be repurposed as a naming convention for a new class of LLM self-reflection mechanisms that intentionally retain inconsistent, unedited past reasoning trajectories in their dynamic memory, unlike polished standard reflection methods that discard flawed steps. These 'reflexion methods' leverage minor 'imperfections' in stored memory to enable the multiple-perspective self-reflection that cutting-edge LLM reflection research finds improves reasoning performance.
